error_signature: Invalid domain: must be a private top-level domain
title: Invalid domain for Google API OAuth2 authorization
symptom: When setting up Google Drive credentials, an error message appears stating 'Invalid domain: must be a private top-level domain'. This prevents successful configuration.
applies_when: Setting authorization domain in Google Cloud Console
root_cause: Google's OAuth2 requires a valid top-level domain for authorization. The provided domain includes subdomains, which are not acceptable for this purpose.
fix_steps: Identify the top-level domain from your current subdomain (e.g., hstgr.cloud)
Enter the top-level domain in the Google Cloud Console's Branding section
Save the changes and retry the OAuth2 setup
agent_instruction: Use the top-level domain 'hstgr.cloud' for the authorization domain in Google Cloud Console.
